Modules » Mesh & Geometry

 Internal parser for conversion/aux I/O.

 Mesh nodes (owned)

 Mesh triangles (owned)
 
std::vector< edge > edges
 Unique edges discovered from triangles.
 
std::set< int > domainIndeces
 All domain IDs appearing on any triangle.
 
std::vector< rvec * > supNodes
 Supplementary nodes for boundary conditions.
 
std::vector< Triangle * > supTriangles
 Supplementary triangles for boundary conditions.
 
std::vector< SymmetryOperation * > bcs
 Boundary conditions (symmetry operations) used to detect periodic edges.
 

Detailed Description

Lightweight surface mesh container with file I/O and BC helpers.

Responsibilities:

  • Store nodes, triangles, and unique edges (each geometric edge appears once).
  • Read meshes from several formats (.mesh, .mphtxt, .msh) and write .mesh.
  • Provide edge queries (index/length/vector) and triangle lists per domain.
  • Handle periodic boundary conditions via stored symmetry operations.

Definition at line 44 of file SurfaceMesh.h.

Constructor & Destructor Documentation

◆ SurfaceMesh()

SurfaceMesh::SurfaceMesh ( )

Default constructor.

Definition at line 14 of file SurfaceMesh.cpp.

◆ ~SurfaceMesh()

SurfaceMesh::~SurfaceMesh ( )
virtual

Destructor: frees BC ops and supplementary nodes/triangles.

Destructor: free symmetry ops and supplementary mesh entities.

Frees:

  • all SymmetryOperation* stored in bcs
  • supplementary nodes added via AddNode()
  • supplementary triangles added via AddTriangle()

Definition at line 24 of file SurfaceMesh.cpp.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ ConvertMeshFile()

int SurfaceMesh::ConvertMeshFile ( std::string inputMeshFile)

Convert/modify a mesh according to a conversion job file.

Recognized blocks:

  • \(<mesh>\)file \(</mesh>\) : load a mesh
  • \(<index>\)n1 n2 \(</index>\) : SwitchIndex(n1,n2)
  • \(<plane>...</plane>\) : RemovePlane(normal, point)
  • \(<out>\)base \(</out>\) : SaveToMeshFile(base + ".mesh")
Parameters
inputMeshFilePath to conversion job file.
Returns
0 on success.

Convert/modify a mesh according to a conversion job file.

Recognized directives:

  • \(<mesh>\)file \(</mesh>\) : LoadFromFile(file)
  • \(<index>\)n1 n2 \(</index>\) : SwitchIndex(n1, n2)
  • \(<plane>\)... \(</plane>\) : RemovePlane(normal, point)
  • \(<out>\)base \(</out>\) : SaveToMeshFile(base + ".mesh")

◆ LoadFromFile()

int SurfaceMesh::LoadFromFile ( std::string fileName)

Load a mesh from file by extension.

Dispatches to:

  • LoadFromMphtxtFile() for ".mphtxt"
  • LoadFromMshFile() for ".msh"
  • LoadFromMeshFile() otherwise (custom .mesh)
Parameters
fileNamePath to input mesh file.
Returns
0 on success; non-zero on error.

Definition at line 37 of file SurfaceMesh.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ LoadFromMeshFile()

int SurfaceMesh::LoadFromMeshFile ( std::string fileName)

Read mesh from the custom ".mesh" text format.

Parameters
fileNamePath to .mesh file.
Returns
0 on success; non-zero on error.

Read custom ".mesh" format: nodes, then triangles (+ domains), detect edges.

Expected layout: \(<Nnodes>\) x y z (repeated Nnodes times) \(<Ntris>\) n1 n2 n3 d1 d2 (1-based node indices, two bordering domains)

Definition at line 59 of file SurfaceMesh.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ LoadFromMphtxtFile()

int SurfaceMesh::LoadFromMphtxtFile ( std::string fileName)

Read mesh from a COMSOL ".mphtxt" file.

Parameters
fileNamePath to .mphtxt file.
Returns
0 on success; non-zero on error.

Read mesh from a COMSOL 4.2 ".mphtxt" file and border-domain block.

Reads the node block, then the triangle connectivity (node indices), then the bordering domain pairs (d1,d2). Edges are detected uniquely.

Definition at line 129 of file SurfaceMesh.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ LoadFromMshFile()

int SurfaceMesh::LoadFromMshFile ( std::string fileName)

Read mesh from a Gmsh ".msh" file (v2 text).

Parameters
fileNamePath to .msh file.
Returns
0 on success; non-zero on error.

Read mesh from a Gmsh ".msh" file and an auxiliary conversion job.

Uses JobParser to optionally map element IDs to (dom1,dom2) pairs via a \(<boundary>\) block inside the currently open job file (external to the .msh).
